task_completion.description: A scatter plot of the 15 data points is created with GNI per capita on the x-axis and life expectancy on the y-axis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A coordinate plane in GeoGebra showing exactly 15 points trending upwards. The x-axis should reflect large GNI values (e.g., 1,000 to 80,000+) and the y-axis should reflect life expectancy values (e.g., 50 to 90).

task_completion.description: A linear regression model is fitted to the scatter plot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A straight line passing through the scatter plot points with a positive slope, visible in the GeoGebra Graphics view.

task_completion.description: A logarithmic regression model is fitted to the scatter plot.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A curved line (concave down) passing through the scatter plot points, showing a steeper slope at lower x-values and flattening out at higher x-values, visible in the GeoGebra Graphics view.

task_completion.description: Text labels displaying the equations for both regression models are present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text boxes on the Graphics view or explicit equation entries in the Algebra view (left panel) showing the mathematical formulas for both the linear (y = mx + b) and logarithmic (y = a ln(x) + b) fits.

task_completion.description: The project is saved as world_regression.ggb in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra window title bar displays 'world_regression.ggb', or a save dialog box is visible showing the file being saved as 'world_regression.ggb' in the '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to create the visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the actual GeoGebra application interface (Algebra view, Graphics view, Spreadsheet view) being interacted with, rather than a Python script (like matplotlib) or terminal generating the plot.

integrity.description: The scatter plot reflects the actual dataset, not randomly generated points.
integrity.visual_evidence: The points should show a realistic distribution with diminishing returns (a curve shape) rather than a perfectly straight line or completely random noise.

integrity.description: The .ggb file was saved via the application, not mocked via terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction with GeoGebra's 'File -> Save' menu or save dialogs, rather than terminal commands like 'touch world_regression.ggb' or 'cp'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains exactly 15 data points representing 15 countries. The linear regression model has a positive slope (approximately 0.000283). The logarithmic regression model is a better fit (R² ≈ 0.687) than the linear model (R² ≈ 0.551). The expected output filename is 'world_regression.ggb'.
